역대상 5:1.content: The sons of Reuben the firstborn of Israel (for he was the firstborn; but, because he defiled his father’s couch, his birthright was given to the sons of Joseph the son of Israel; and the genealogy is not to be listed according to the birthright.

역대상 5:2.content: For Judah prevailed above his brothers, and from him came the prince; but the birthright was Joseph’s)—

역대상 5:3.content: the sons of Reuben the firstborn of Israel: Hanoch, Pallu, Hezron, and Carmi.

역대상 5:4.content: The sons of Joel: Shemaiah his son, Gog his son, Shimei his son,

역대상 5:5.content: Micah his son, Reaiah his son, Baal his son,

역대상 5:6.content: and Beerah his son, whom Tilgath Pilneser king of Assyria carried away captive. He was prince of the Reubenites.

역대상 5:7.content: His brothers by their families, when the genealogy of their generations was listed: the chief, Jeiel, and Zechariah,

역대상 5:8.content: and Bela the son of Azaz, the son of Shema, the son of Joel, who lived in Aroer, even to Nebo and Baal Meon;

역대상 5:9.content: and he lived eastward even to the entrance of the wilderness from the river Euphrates, because their livestock were multiplied in the land of Gilead.

역대상 5:10.content: In the days of Saul, they made war with the Hagrites, who fell by their hand; and they lived in their tents throughout all the land east of Gilead.

역대상 5:11.content: The sons of Gad lived beside them, in the land of Bashan to Salecah:

역대상 5:12.content: Joel the chief, Shapham the second, Janai, and Shaphat in Bashan.

역대상 5:13.content: Their brothers of their fathers’ houses: Michael, Meshullam, Sheba, Jorai, Jacan, Zia, and Eber, seven.

역대상 5:14.content: These were the sons of Abihail, the son of Huri, the son of Jaroah, the son of Gilead, the son of Michael, the son of Jeshishai, the son of Jahdo, the son of Buz;

역대상 5:15.content: Ahi the son of Abdiel, the son of Guni, chief of their fathers’ houses.

역대상 5:16.content: They lived in Gilead in Bashan, and in its towns, and in all the pasture lands of Sharon, as far as their borders.

역대상 5:17.content: All these were listed by genealogies in the days of Jotham king of Judah, and in the days of Jeroboam king of Israel.

역대상 5:18.content: The sons of Reuben, the Gadites, and the half-tribe of Manasseh, of valiant men, men able to bear buckler and sword, and to shoot with bow, and skillful in war, were forty-four thousand seven hundred sixty, that were able to go out to war.

역대상 5:19.content: They made war with the Hagrites, with Jetur, and Naphish, and Nodab.

역대상 5:20.content: They were helped against them, and the Hagrites were delivered into their hand, and all who were with them; for they cried to God in the battle, and he answered them, because they put their trust in him.

역대상 5:21.content: They took away their livestock; of their camels fifty thousand, and of sheep two hundred fifty thousand, and of donkeys two thousand, and of men one hundred thousand.

역대상 5:22.content: For many fell slain, because the war was of God. They lived in their place until the captivity.

역대상 5:23.content: The children of the half-tribe of Manasseh lived in the land: they increased from Bashan to Baal Hermon, Senir, and Mount Hermon.

역대상 5:24.content: These were the heads of their fathers’ houses: even Epher, Ishi, Eliel, Azriel, Jeremiah, Hodaviah, and Jahdiel, mighty men of valor, famous men, heads of their fathers’ houses.

역대상 5:25.content: They trespassed against the God of their fathers, and played the prostitute after the gods of the peoples of the land, whom God destroyed before them.

역대상 5:26.content: So the God of Israel stirred up the spirit of Pul king of Assyria, and the spirit of Tilgath Pilneser king of Assyria, and he carried them away, even the Reubenites, and the Gadites, and the half-tribe of Manasseh, and brought them to Halah, Habor, Hara, and to the river of Gozan, to this day.
